scenarios.description: Verify that a new user can register successfully with valid email and password.
Verify successful registration when password is exactly the minimum allowed length of 6 characters.
Verify that registration is rejected with 409 Conflict when attempting to register an already existing email.
Verify registration fails with 400 when the password is below the minimum length of 6 characters.
Verify registration fails with 400 when the required email field is missing.
Verify registration fails with 400 when the required password field is missing.
Verify registration fails with 400 when the email format is invalid.
Verify registration fails with 400 when the email value is explicitly null.
Verify registration fails with 400 when the password value is explicitly null.
Verify registration fails with 400 when request body is an empty JSON object.
Verify registration is successful for complex but valid email formats containing subdomains and aliases.
Successfully authenticates a user with valid email and password, returning a JWT token.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login with password at minimum length boundary
scenarios.description: Attempts login with a password of exactly 6 characters. Validation passes, returns 401 due to invalid credentials.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login fails with password below minimum length
scenarios.description: Attempts login with a password shorter than 6 characters, expecting a 400 validation error.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login fails with missing password field
scenarios.description: Sends login request with missing password field, expecting a 400 validation error.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login fails with missing email field
scenarios.description: Sends login request with missing email field, expecting a 400 validation error.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login fails with malformed email format
scenarios.description: Sends an email address missing the @ symbol, expecting a 400 validation error.
Attempts to log in with a valid-format email that does not exist in the database, expecting 401.
Attempts to log in with a correct username but incorrect password. Expected to fail with 401.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login with extremely long valid email format
scenarios.description: Sends a maximum-length valid email format. Validation should pass and return 401 (unauthorized) instead of 400.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login fails with empty request body
scenarios.description: Sends an empty JSON object, expecting 400 validation error due to missing required fields.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login fails with spaces in email address
scenarios.description: Sends an email address containing whitespace, expecting a 400 validation error.

scenarios.scenarioName: Login fails with incorrect field data types
scenarios.description: Sends non-string values for email (boolean) and password (integer), expecting 400 validation error.
Create a category by providing both a unique name and a valid description string.
Create a category by providing only the required name field, omitting description.
Create a category with a valid unique name and an empty string as the description.
Create a category with a long but valid name string to check length boundary handling.
Attempt to create a category without providing the required name field, expecting validation failure.
Attempt to create a category without providing an Authorization header, expecting a 401 response.
Attempt to create a category using an invalid or malformed bearer token, expecting a 401 response.
Attempt to create a category with a null value for the name field, expecting validation failure.
Attempt to create a category with an empty string as the name, expecting validation failure.
Attempt to send a JSON array instead of a JSON object for the category details, expecting a bad request.

scenarios.scenarioName: Create category with invalid description type
scenarios.description: Attempt to create a category with an integer description instead of a string, expecting validation failure.
Successfully create a new product with valid name, price, stock, and category ID.

scenarios.scenarioName: Create product with minimum valid price
scenarios.description: Verify boundary condition where product price is exactly 0.

scenarios.scenarioName: Create product with minimum valid stock
scenarios.description: Verify boundary condition where product stock is exactly 0.
Attempt to create a product without providing an authentication token.
Attempt to create a product with name omitted from the payload.
Attempt to create a product with price omitted from the payload.
Attempt to create a product with stock omitted from the payload.
Attempt to create a product with categoryId omitted from the payload.
Attempt to create a product with a string-typed price.
Attempt to create a product with a floating-point number instead of an integer for stock.
Attempt to create a product with a negative price value, violating minimum constraints.
Attempt to create a product with a negative stock value, violating minimum constraints.
Attempt to create a product where categoryId is an array instead of a string.
Successfully update category name and description with valid JWT authentication.
Verify category can be updated with a single character name.
Verify category can be updated with a long name of 255 characters.
Verify category can be updated with an empty string for the optional description field.
Verify description field can be completely omitted from payload as it is not required.
Verify update fails with 400 when the required name field is missing from payload.
Verify CUD modification fails with 401 when Authorization header is missing.
Verify CUD modification fails with 401 when using Basic instead of Bearer token.
Verify CUD modification fails with 401 when Bearer token is empty.
Verify update fails with 400 when the name field is sent as an integer type.
Verify update fails with 400 when the description field is sent as a boolean.
Verify update fails with 400 when sending an empty JSON object.
Successfully registers a new user account with unique credentials.
Fails to register when using an already registered email address.
Authenticates successfully with valid credentials and extracts JWT token.
Rejects authentication attempts with incorrect credentials.
Creates a new category under authenticated context.
Rejects category creation when no authentication token is provided.
Creates a new product associated with an existing category.
Verifies product creation with minimum price and stock values (0).
Successfully updates category details.
Successfully updates product details with valid credentials.
Validates update operation when price and stock are set to boundary minimum (0).
Rejects product update requests when required body properties are missing.
Retrieves a list of all existing categories.
Retrieves a list of all existing products.
Deletes a product by ID under authenticated context.
Deletes a category after associated products have been removed.
Retrieves the list of all categories as an anonymous user, ensuring the endpoint is publicly accessible.
Ensures the GET categories endpoint functions correctly and returns 200 OK when a valid Bearer token is provided.

scenarios.scenarioName: Verify newly created category in list
scenarios.description: Verifies the list of categories can be retrieved successfully after a new category has been created.
Verify that the products list can be retrieved successfully without any authentication.

scenarios.scenarioName: Retrieve products with JSON accept header
scenarios.description: Verify retrieval of products list with explicit Accept: application/json header.
Verify request is rejected with 401 when an invalid/malformed Bearer token is supplied.

scenarios.scenarioName: Retrieve products ignoring unexpected query params
scenarios.description: Verify GET /api/products succeeds when passing unrecognized query parameters.
Delete an existing category by ID using a valid bearer token.
Attempt to delete a category without providing an authorization token.
Attempt to delete a category providing an invalid malformed bearer token.
Attempt to delete a category using a non-existent ID string.
Attempt to delete a category with whitespace characters as ID path parameter.

scenarios.scenarioName: Delete category with extremely long ID
scenarios.description: Attempt to delete a category with an extremely long path parameter string.

scenarios.scenarioName: Delete category with special characters in ID
scenarios.description: Attempt to delete a category where ID parameter contains special characters.

scenarios.scenarioName: Delete category with invalid request body schema
scenarios.description: Provide an invalid JSON object body where string schema is expected.

scenarios.scenarioName: Delete category with empty string request body
scenarios.description: Provide an empty string JSON body payload to test schema boundaries.
Attempt to delete a category that has already been successfully deleted.

scenarios.scenarioName: Delete category with random UUID string
scenarios.description: Attempt to delete a category using a randomly generated UUID format.
